name: Priory of Saint John Trail
description: This easy 3.9-mile (6.2 km) circular hike in Argamasilla de Alba explores historical sites like the Church of San Juan Bautista.
region_intro: Hiking around Argamasilla De Alba offers access to the unique landscapes of the Lagunas de Ruidera Natural Park. This region is characterized by a chain of 15 stepped lakes interconnected by waterfalls and streams, forming a distinct aquatic environment in the Upper Guadiana Valley. The terrain varies from mostly accessible paths around the lagoons to more demanding routes with elevation changes, often featuring views of historical landmarks like Peñarroya Castle. Best hiking trails around Argamasilla De Alba The most popular hiking route is Peñarroya Castle and Reservoir Loop, a 5.2 miles (8.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 16 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of the historic castle and the reservoir. Another top favourite among local hikers is Embalse Peñarroya from the Castillo de Peñarroya — round trip via GR-114, a difficult 15.8 miles (25.5 km) path. This longer trail provides extensive views of the reservoir and surrounding natural features. Local hikers also love the Priory of Saint John Trail, a 3.9 miles (6.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 35 minutes. Hiking around Argamasilla De Alba is defined by stepped lakes, interconnected waterfalls, and historical fortresses. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging long-distance routes. The routes in Argamasilla De Alba are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from more than 160 reviews. More than 1100 hikers have used komoot to explore Argamasilla De Alba's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Argamasilla De Alba?
A: There are over 80 hiking routes available around Argamasilla De Alba, catering to various skill levels. These include 46 easy routes, 30 moderate routes, and 5 more challenging trails.

Q: What kind of natural features can I expect to see while hiking in Argamasilla De Alba?
A: The region is renowned for the unique landscapes of the Lagunas de Ruidera Natural Park, featuring a spectacular chain of 15 stepped lakes interconnected by waterfalls and streams. You can explore highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5573792" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna del Rey&lt;/a&gt;, known for its clear waters, and various waterfalls such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3844258" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Batanas Lagoon Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5884091" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Hundimiento Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical landmarks or points of interest along the hiking routes?
A: Yes, many routes offer views of historical landmarks. A prominent example is &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1207403009/penarroya-castle-and-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñarroya Castle&lt;/a&gt;, an ancient fortress that houses the Sanctuary of Nuestra Señora de Peñarroya. The region's connection to the Ruta del Quijote also adds a cultural dimension to your outdoor experience.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Argamasilla De Alba?
A: The Lagunas de Ruidera Natural Park, which is central to hiking in Argamasilla de Alba, offers pleasant conditions for hiking during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder. Summer can be very hot, making early mornings or late afternoons preferable, while winter offers a different, often quieter, perspective on the landscapes.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking options available?
A: Yes, there are 46 easy routes suitable for families and beginners. The terrain around the lagoons often features mostly accessible paths. An example of an easy trail is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1207382631/priory-of-saint-john-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Priory of Saint John Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.9 miles (6.2 km) long and takes about 1 hour 35 minutes to complete.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Argamasilla De Alba?
A: Many trails in natural parks and rural areas are generally dog-friendly, especially if your dog is well-behaved and kept on a leash. However, it's always advisable to check specific regulations for the Lagunas de Ruidera Natural Park regarding pet access, especially in protected areas or near wildlife.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the area?
A: Yes, circular routes are available, allowing you to start and end your hike at the same point. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1207403009/penarroya-castle-and-reservoir-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñarroya Castle and Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate circular trail, covering 5.2 miles (8.4 km) and typically taking around 2 hours 16 minutes.

Q: What are the typical durations and distances for hikes in Argamasilla De Alba?
A: Hikes vary significantly in duration and distance. You can find shorter, easy walks like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1207382631/priory-of-saint-john-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Priory of Saint John Trail&lt;/a&gt; at 3.9 miles (6.2 km) taking under two hours, to much longer and more challenging routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1373881548/embalse-penarroya-from-the-castillo-de-penarroya-round-trip-via-gr-114" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse Peñarroya from the Castillo de Peñarroya — round trip via GR-114&lt;/a&gt;, which is 15.8 miles (25.5 km) and can take several hours to complete.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Argamasilla De Alba?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 160 reviews. Hikers often praise the stunning natural beauty of the interconnected lagoons and waterfalls, the varied terrain, and the opportunity to combine nature exploration with historical sites like Peñarroya Castle.

Q: Is parking available near the trailheads?
A: Given the popularity of the Lagunas de Ruidera Natural Park and its attractions, parking facilities are generally available at key access points and near popular trailheads. It's recommended to arrive early during peak seasons to secure a spot, especially near attractions like Laguna del Rey or Peñarroya Castle.

Q: Are there any routes that feature the source of the Guadiana River?
A: Yes, the region is known for the Spring of the Lagunas de Ruidera, a designated natural monument where the crystal-clear waters originate, feeding into the Alto Guadiana. While specific routes might not be named after it, many trails within the Lagunas de Ruidera Natural Park will allow you to experience the unique hydrological system that forms the source of the Guadiana River.
tips:
Beautiful square, located in front of the town hall of Argamasilla de Alba.
Very well equipped and well landscaped, it has robust trees.
Decorated with statues and monuments that honor the work "Don Quixote de La Mancha" and also the writer "Azorín".

The infant Sabastián Gabriel de Borbón, Prior of Castile in the Order of San Juan, proposes to build a canal that, collecting the surplus water from the Tuidera Lagoons, will irrigate the lands of the municipalities from Argamasilla de Alba to Arenas de San Juan.

The warehouses are an institution founded in the late Middle Ages. Its main activity was the subsidization of bread in years of famine in order to guarantee its price, as well as bakery among walkers and the poor. To these functions is added, from s. XVII, the loan of wheat or money between farmers. Its maximum splendor occurs throughout the s. XVIII, producing its total disappearance at the end of the s. XX.

Currently this unique space is eventually used as an auditorium.
The church of San Juan Bautista began to be built in 1542 by Juan de Ornero. In 1587 the work was entrusted to the master stonemason Juan de Rigos, who had to complete it in six years. However, for unknown reasons what should have been its main entrance was left unfinished, whose structure, column and arches we have been able to see in the discovery.

faqs.question: How long does it take to complete this circular hike?
faqs.answer: This easy circular hike typically takes around 1 hour and 35 minutes to complete, making it a perfect short stroll.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families with children?
faqs.answer: Yes, absolutely! This route is classified as easy, with minimal elevation gain, making it ideal for beginners, families, and those looking for a relaxed walk.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Argamasilla de Alba circular route?
faqs.answer: As this route explores the town, you can generally find street parking available in Argamasilla de Alba. Starting points are flexible, but parking near the Plaza de España or other central areas would be convenient.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Argamasilla de Alba circular route?
faqs.answer: The route takes you through the heart of Argamasilla de Alba, showcasing several historical and cultural points of interest. You'll pass by the central Plaza de España, the historic Canal of the Grand Prior, the impressive La Tercia Granary, and the beautiful Church of San Juan Bautista.

faqs.question: Is the Argamasilla de Alba circular trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, dogs are generally welcome on this route. Given it's an urban walk, please keep your dog on a leash and be mindful of other pedestrians and local regulations.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this hike?
faqs.answer: No, there are no permits or entrance fees required to enjoy this circular route through Argamasilla de Alba. It's freely accessible to everyone.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The best times to enjoy this trail are spring and autumn, when the weather is mild and pleasant for walking. Summer can be very hot in this region, so if you hike then, it's best to go early in the morning or late in the afternoon.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this circular route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is predominantly urban, consisting of paved streets and sidewalks as you explore the town of Argamasilla de Alba. It's a very accessible and comfortable walk.

faqs.question: Does this trail intersect with any other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly overlaps with sections of the Sendero Priorato de San Juan, the Camino Natural del Guadiana, and the Cañada Real de Cuenca o Vereda Soriana.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the route?
faqs.answer: Absolutely! As the route passes through the town center of Argamasilla de Alba, you'll find various cafes, bars, and restaurants where you can stop for refreshments or a meal.

faqs.question: What should I bring for this easy circular hike?
faqs.answer: For this short and easy urban walk, comfortable walking shoes, water, and sun protection (hat, sunscreen) are recommended, especially during warmer months. A camera would also be great for capturing the historical sights.
